identity.name: Prisma Browser — Sideloaded Risky Extension Install (T1176)
identity.summary: A user attempts to sideload an unsigned Chrome extension whose manifest declares overbroad permissions — `<all_urls>` host access plus `cookies`, `webRequest`, and `webRequestBlocking`. Managed Prisma Browser extension policy blocks the install and logs the attempt; a KOI code scan independently flags the manifest, and the extension permission risk score exceeds threshold.
identity.description: Malicious browser extensions are a durable persistence and data-theft foothold: with `<all_urls>` + `cookies` + `webRequest(Blocking)` an extension can read every page, exfiltrate session cookies, and rewrite traffic. This scenario sideloads the `ai-page-summarizer` extension from the cortex-malicious-agentic-pack, whose `manifest.json` declares exactly those overbroad permissions. The cortex-browser-attacker EAL plugin drives the managed Prisma Browser to attempt the install; the managed-extension policy blocks it and emits a `prisma_browser_events` record with `action=extension-install`, `extension_id`, the declared permission set, a permission risk score, and `outcome=blocked`. In parallel a KOI code scan of the artifact tree flags the manifest's broad scopes and the unapproved publisher signature. Detection layers: a BIOC on the policy-blocked sideload; analytics on permission risk score over threshold; KOI analytics on manifest scope and publisher signature; and a JSONL outcome=blocked delivery check. A correlation rule fuses the policy-block BIOC with the risk-score analytic into one persistence-attempt incident.

prerequisites.description: Managed Prisma Browser with managed-extension policy enforced; reporting telemetry to XSIAM.
Lab environment matching scenario SIM-BROWSER-003 (cortex-browser-attacker + cortex-malicious-agentic-pack present).

payload.code:
# Inspect the manifest, then attempt the sideload through the managed Prisma Browser.
jq '.permissions, .host_permissions' sources/cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/chrome/ai-page-summarizer/manifest.json
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/sim-browser-003-eal.yml --live
# Campaign: navigate chrome://extensions ; install_extension ai-page-summarizer (canary CANARY-EXT-001) -> expect blocked

expected_artifacts.description: Extension-install attempt blocked by managed-extension policy.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: action = "extension-install" and outcome = "blocked" and extension_id != null

expected_artifacts.description: Extension manifest declaring overbroad host + API permissions.

biocs.name: Prisma Browser — sideloaded extension blocked by managed-extension policy
biocs.description: Prisma Browser — sideloaded extension blocked by managed-extension policy. An unsigned/sideloaded extension install attempt is denied by managed-browser policy and logged with outcome=blocked.
biocs.logic:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter action = "extension-install"
| filter install_source in ("sideload", "crx", "developer-mode")
| filter outcome = "blocked"
| filter extension_id != null

xql_queries.name: Code scan — extension manifest declares <all_urls> + cookies + webRequest + webRequestBlocking
xql_queries.query:
dataset = koi_code_scan_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter artifact_type = "browser-extension"
| filter manifest_host_permissions contains "<all_urls>"
| filter manifest_permissions contains "cookies" and manifest_permissions contains "webRequest"
| fields _time, artifact_path, extension_id, manifest_host_permissions, manifest_permissions
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — extension permission risk score exceeds threshold
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "extension-install"
| filter extension_permission_risk_score >= 70
| fields _time, user, extension_id, extension_permission_risk_score, outcome
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: KOI — extension manifest signature does not match approved publisher list
xql_queries.query:
dataset = koi_code_scan_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter artifact_type = "browser-extension"
| filter publisher_approved = false or signature_valid = false
| fields _time, extension_id, publisher, publisher_approved, signature_valid
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: Browser-attacker JSONL records install_extension outcome=blocked
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter campaign_id = "BC-BROWSER-003"
| filter action = "extension-install" and outcome = "blocked"
| fields _time, campaign_id, action, extension_id, outcome, cortex_canary
| sort desc _time

correlation_rules.name: Browser Risky Extension Sideload — Overbroad Permissions
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Prisma Browser — sideloaded extension blocked by managed-extension policy)

analytics_modules: Prisma Browser — Extension permission risk scoring
Cortex KOI — Browser-extension manifest + publisher-signature analysis

products.evidence_query: dataset = prisma_browser_events | filter action = "extension-install" and outcome = "blocked" | fields _time, user, extension_id
dataset = koi_code_scan_events | filter artifact_type = "browser-extension" and manifest_host_permissions contains "<all_urls>"
Correlation CR-BROWSER-0003 raises a persistence-attempt incident on a blocked risky-extension sideload.

use_cases.name: Managed-Browser Extension Risk Policy
use_cases.description: Block and detect sideloaded extensions with overbroad permissions in the managed Prisma Browser, with KOI manifest + publisher corroboration.

test_cases.objective: Overbroad-permission sideload is blocked and logged by Prisma Browser policy.
preconditions: Managed Prisma Browser with extension policy enforced.
steps: Run SIM-BROWSER-003 step-01 (manifest scan) and step-02 (sideload attempt).
Observe the blocked-install alert and KOI manifest finding in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `Prisma Browser — sideloaded extension blocked by managed-extension policy` fires within 60s.
Permission-risk-score and KOI manifest XQL queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Policy block and risk-score analytic stitch into one incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-BROWSER-0003` produces a single persistence-attempt incident.

preventive_controls: Enforce a managed-extension allowlist in Prisma Browser; block all sideloaded/developer-mode installs.
Deny extensions requesting <all_urls> host access combined with cookies/webRequest unless explicitly vetted.
Require approved-publisher signatures on every installed extension.
detection_engineering: Alert on any extension-install attempt with a permission risk score over threshold or outcome=blocked.
Scan extension manifests for the <all_urls> + cookies + webRequest(Blocking) anti-pattern via KOI code scan.
Hunt for extensions whose publisher signature is absent or not on the approved list.
